scaffolds.q: Pain is detected by specialized receptors in the body.
scaffolds.explain: Pain is detected by nociceptors — free nerve endings that respond to stimuli intense enough to cause or threaten tissue damage. Keep this momentum: now: they're your body's alarm system for 'this could kill you.'

Nociceptors (from Latin 'nocere' meaning 'to harm') detect potentially harmful stimuli — extreme temperatures that burn tissue, intense pressure that crushes cells, damaging chemicals that dissolve membranes. Look at what occurs:, darling: they're not temperature sensors. They're DAMAGE sensors.
